# Story: Bugfix 10.3 — Homepage Skeleton Re-mount/Flicker on Load (SPIKE-GATED)

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
<!-- Generated by SM Bob /create-story (YOLO) on 2026-05-07. -->
<!-- ⚠️ SPIKE-GATED STORY: Task 1 is a ≤30 min React DevTools Profiler diagnosis.
     Outcome dictates whether Tasks 2-6 (the fix) execute or get reduced to a
     project-context.md note. Do NOT skip Task 1's halt-and-decide gate. -->

## Story

As a user landing on the homepage at `/`,
I want the four homepage sections (HeroBanner, ExploreBlocksList, RecentMediaPanel, DownloadPanel) to settle into their final state without their loading skeletons flashing or re-mounting more than once,
so that the first-paint experience feels intentional instead of "the page is broken / something is reloading", and so that perceived performance stops competing with the LCP win that Story 10-5 lazy-loading already delivered.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **[AC #1 — SPIKE OUTCOME, BLOCKING ALL OTHER ACs]** Given a 30-minute React DevTools Profiler session executed at HEAD on the `/` route in `pnpm nx serve web` (dev) AND `pnpm nx run web:preview` (prod build), when the profiler trace is recorded across full page-load + idle, then the spike MUST classify each observed skeleton transition into exactly one bucket and document it in `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/spike-bugfix-10-3-findings.md`:
   - **Bucket A — `dev-mode-only`:** Confirmed React 18 StrictMode double-mount (`apps/web/src/main.tsx:11`) artifact. Production preview build does NOT exhibit the flicker. → Fix is documentation-only (Tasks 2-6 collapse; see AC #10).
   - **Bucket B — `real-prod-bug`:** Flicker reproduces in `web:preview` (production bundle, no StrictMode). At least one specific transition is identified with the offending component + render trigger. → Tasks 2-6 execute against the identified transitions.
   - **Bucket C — `mixed`:** StrictMode amplifies a real but minor prod transition. → Tasks 2-6 execute, scope narrowed to ONLY the prod-reproducing transitions.
   - The spike doc MUST list every transition observed (component, before-state, after-state, render count, total ms) so a future regression test has a baseline.

2. **[@contract-v1, applies if AC #1 = Bucket B or C]** Given the homepage `/` route on the production preview build, when the user lands cold (no warm cache, page reload), then between first paint and "all data settled" each of the four sections (`hero-banner-skeleton`, `explore-blocks-loading`, RecentMediaPanel skeleton, DownloadPanel skeleton) MUST transition from skeleton → final-state AT MOST ONCE. A second skeleton render after the first paint of real content for the same section is a regression. Validated by AC #6's React Testing Library render-count assertion + AC #7's E2E Playwright trace.

3. **[applies if AC #1 = Bucket B or C]** Given the `useInViewport` hook at `apps/web/src/hooks/useInViewport.ts:31`, when a lazy `ExploreBlock` mounts above-the-fold (i.e. the block is in viewport AT mount time), then `isInViewport` MUST resolve to `true` synchronously on first render — not on a follow-up `setIsInViewport(true)` triggered by the effect. The current `useState(disabled)` initializer returns `false` for `disabled=false` blocks even when they ARE in viewport, forcing a 1-render skeleton flash before the IntersectionObserver callback fires. Use a lazy initializer or `useLayoutEffect`-coordinated initial check; jsdom/SSR fallback at line 42-46 must keep working unchanged.

4. **[applies if AC #1 = Bucket B or C]** Given `ExploreBlocksList` at `apps/web/src/components/homepage/ExploreBlocksList.tsx:46-54`, when `useExploreBlocks` flips from `isLoading` to `data` available, then the outer `min-h-[200px]` placeholder MUST hand off to the inner `ExploreBlocksListInner` WITHOUT a frame where the placeholder collapses to zero height before the children mount (no layout-jitter window). If a `<div className="min-h-[200px]">` wrapper around the inner list satisfies this, prefer it over a portal/lift solution.

5. **[applies if AC #1 = Bucket B or C]** Given the section-level loading skeletons (`HeroBanner` line 169-178, `ExploreBlockSkeleton`, RecentMediaPanel, DownloadPanel), when the parent's `isLoading` state flips to `false` AND the data array is empty / hidden (AC #5 of Story 10-5), then the skeleton MUST NOT render even one frame before the empty/hidden branch wins. Verify each component's render order: empty/hidden check should short-circuit BEFORE the `if (isLoading)` branch returns the skeleton, OR the `isLoading` branch must return `null` when the parent already knows the data is empty.

6. **[applies if AC #1 = Bucket B or C]** Given regression coverage for the no-flicker invariant, when running `nx test web`, then a new `apps/web/src/components/homepage/__no-flicker.spec.tsx` (or co-located per-component additions) MUST assert: for each of the 4 homepage sections, **render count ≤ N** (where N is the per-component baseline established by the spike — typically 3 for hooks-driven components: initial → mount-effect → settled) and the SAME `data-testid` (e.g. `explore-block-${id}`) MUST NOT toggle between rendered/not-rendered/rendered within a single act() flush. Use `vi.fn` render counters wired into a test wrapper or `@testing-library/react`'s `rerender` snapshots.

7. **[applies if AC #1 = Bucket B or C]** Given an E2E Playwright spec under `tests/e2e/homepage-no-flicker.spec.ts`, when navigating to `/` with mocked TMDb + ExploreBlocks + Recent + Downloads endpoints (deterministic delays of 100ms each), then for each `data-testid` listed in AC #2 the test MUST assert that the testid appears AT MOST ONCE per "category" — i.e. the skeleton testid (`hero-banner-skeleton`, `explore-blocks-loading`, etc.) appears, then the final-state testid (`hero-banner`, `explore-blocks-list`) appears, and the skeleton does NOT re-appear after the final-state lands. Use `page.locator(...).count()` snapshots before + after `networkidle` to catch flickers a `toBeVisible()` chain would miss.

8. **[applies if AC #1 = Bucket B or C]** Given the `tmdbIds` ownership-recompute gate at `ExploreBlocksList.tsx:97-99` (`anyEnabledInflight`), when a lazy block reveals AND its content query is mid-flight, then ownership recompute MUST stay deferred (no PosterCard `isOwned`/`isRequested` flip-flop). The existing `anyEnabledInflight` guard already documents this; verify it still holds after AC #3-5 changes by adding ONE assertion to the existing `useOwnedMedia` test that mounts a 3-block list (1 eager + 2 lazy), simulates lazy reveal, and asserts `mockUseOwnedMedia` (or the underlying POST) fires AT MOST `1 + lazy_block_count` times — not `2 × lazy_block_count`.

9. **[applies always]** Given Rule 12 (CI Lint) + Rule 16 (Test Assertion Quality), when the story lands, then `pnpm nx test web` AND `pnpm lint:all` MUST stay green. ZERO new test placeholders / `as any` casts / `toBeFalsy()`-style weak assertions introduced by AC #6/7. Use `Partial<UseQueryResult<...>>` typing for new mocks (CR M3 precedent from bugfix-10-2).

10. **[applies if AC #1 = Bucket A only]** Given Bucket A (dev-mode-only artifact), when the spike concludes, then the fix collapses to: (a) one paragraph appended to `project-context.md` under a new "Known dev-mode artifacts" section documenting the StrictMode double-mount + linking to the spike doc; (b) sprint-status.yaml entry flipped to `done` with a note that no code changed; (c) close the story with Tasks 2-8 marked `~~[skipped]~~` and a Completion Notes entry referencing AC #10. NO test file is added — adding a regression test for a non-existent prod bug would be permanent dead weight.

11. **Out-of-scope (deferred to follow-ups):** the following are NOT addressed by this story regardless of spike outcome:
    - Refactoring to remove `useInViewport` in favor of a Suspense-based lazy-load (would need React 18 Suspense + transition rework — separate spike).
    - Server-side rendering / streaming HTML for the homepage (would obviate skeletons entirely but blows the deployment model — separate epic).
    - LCP / CLS measurement changes — Story 10-5 owns the LCP contract; AC #2 here is a NO-FLICKER invariant, not a perf regression check.
    - The pre-existing 7 baseline `as any` warnings in `ExploreBlocksList.spec.tsx:94, 101` (filed as bugfix-10-1 follow-up). Touch ONLY if a test added under AC #6 inherently fixes them.

Change the `useState(disabled)` initializer to a lazy initializer that ALSO checks if `ref.current` is already in viewport on first render — e.g. read `ref.current?.getBoundingClientRect()` against `window.innerHeight` + `rootMargin`. Bail-out cleanly if `ref.current` is null (typical first render before ref attaches) so the existing IntersectionObserver fallback still drives the steady-state.

  - [ ] 2.3 Update `apps/web/src/hooks/useInViewport.spec.ts` with one new test: "when ref node is in viewport at first render, returns true synchronously without an extra setState". Use `getBoundingClientRect` mock returning a rect inside `window.innerHeight` and assert `result.current` is `true` on the first render snapshot.

- [ ] **Task 3: Stabilize `ExploreBlocksList` outer→inner handoff (AC: #4) [conditional on Task 1.5 = B/C]**

Wrap `<ExploreBlocksListInner ... />` (line 59) in a `<div className="min-h-[200px]">` that matches the placeholder's reserved height EXACTLY, so the swap from placeholder → inner does not collapse the box even for 0ms. Verify via the existing `explore-blocks-loading` testid behavior (it goes away when `isLoading=false` — that's correct).

- [ ] **Task 4: Short-circuit empty/hidden BEFORE skeleton (AC: #5) [conditional on Task 1.5 = B/C]**

For initial-load with empty trending data, this produces 1 frame of skeleton → 1 frame of nothing. Reorder: if the parent has a way to know empty BEFORE loading completes (typically not for HeroBanner since it's its own data source), the AC is moot. For sections where `hideWhenEmpty` is a prop driven by parent (RecentMediaPanel, DownloadPanel), short-circuit on `hideWhenEmpty && (no data OR empty data)` BEFORE rendering the skeleton.

If `hideWhenEmpty=true` AND there's a fast way to know upstream that the data WILL be empty (e.g. a prior request's empty cache), prefer `return null` over rendering skeleton-then-null. If no such signal exists, document the constraint in a comment and keep the existing order — this AC does not mandate adding new state.

## Dev Notes

### Why this story is SPIKE-GATED

Sprint-status.yaml line 487 explicitly mandates spike-first: *"if dev-mode artifact only → QD documents in project-context and closes. If real prod bug → SM /create-story with full AC + fix."* The user has chosen to bypass `/quick-dev` and produce a full story upfront — but the spike gate is preserved as Task 1 / AC #1. This avoids the failure mode where DEV implements a fix for a phantom bug that only StrictMode reproduces, then ships dead-weight tests.

The spike output (`spike-bugfix-10-3-findings.md`) is the FIRST commit of this story's branch. Tasks 2-6 only execute if the spike justifies them.

### Root-cause hypothesis (to be confirmed/refuted by spike)

Five candidate triggers, ordered by likelihood:

1. **StrictMode double-mount (DEV-ONLY).** `apps/web/src/main.tsx:11` wraps the app in `<StrictMode>`. React 18 intentionally double-invokes effects in dev to surface side-effect-leak bugs. Each `useInViewport`-driven `ExploreBlock` runs its observer-attach effect twice; each `useState(disabled)` resets to `false` between mounts. Net effect: every lazy block flashes its `ExploreBlockSkeleton` an extra time. **Does NOT happen in `web:preview` build** because StrictMode's dev-only behavior is stripped.

2. **`useInViewport` initial-state race (REAL).** `useInViewport.ts:31` initializes `useState(disabled)` — for `disabled=false` (the lazy-block default), this returns `false` even when the node ALREADY is in viewport at mount. The effect on lines 33-71 then runs the `IntersectionObserver` setup; if `IntersectionObserver` is missing (line 42), it sets `isInViewport=true`, but for the normal browser path, the observer's first callback is what flips state — even though the "page-already-loaded-and-block-is-visible" case is the most common. This produces: render 1 → `false` → skeleton; render 2 → IO callback fires → `true` → real content. ONE extra render even outside StrictMode. AC #3 fixes this.

3. **`ExploreBlocksList` outer→inner placeholder swap (REAL, minor).** Lines 46-59 render a `min-h-[200px]` placeholder while `useExploreBlocks` loads. When data arrives, `ExploreBlocksListInner` (line 62) takes over and starts rendering blocks — each block initially in skeleton state. There's NO SHARED CONTAINER between the placeholder and the inner — so for one frame, the placeholder unmounts BEFORE the inner mounts and the layout box collapses. Visually: 200px box → 0px box → child blocks reserving 200px each. AC #4 fixes by wrapping inner in a stable `min-h` container.

4. **Section short-circuit ordering (REAL, minor).** `HeroBanner.tsx:169-183` renders the skeleton (lines 170-178) BEFORE checking `isError || !hasItems` (line 181). For initial-load where `useTrendingHero` returns empty (e.g. TMDb degraded), the user sees: skeleton → null. AC #5 reorders.

5. **`tmdbIds` ownership recompute gate (NOT a flicker issue, but adjacent).** `ExploreBlocksList.tsx:97-99` already gates ownership against `anyEnabledInflight`. Spike should confirm this gate doesn't accidentally release while a child block transitions, which would re-fire `useOwnedMedia` and (theoretically) cause PosterCard `isOwned` flips. The existing memo dependency list looks correct — this is a "verify, don't fix" item. AC #8 adds a regression assertion.

The spike's job is to MEASURE which of these contribute in prod and which are dev-mode noise. Without the spike, we'd risk fixing #1 (impossible — it's a feature of dev) or shipping #2-#4 fixes that don't matter to real users.

- **Rule 1 (Single Backend):** N/A — this is a pure FE story.
- **Rule 5 (TanStack Query for Server State):** All state stays in TanStack Query / `useState` for ephemeral UI state. ZERO Zustand additions.
- **Rule 7 (Error Codes System):** N/A — no new error codes.
- **Rule 12 (CI Lint):** AC #9 enforces `pnpm lint:all` + `nx test web` green.
- **Rule 13 (Error Handling):** N/A — no new error paths.
- **Rule 15 (Pre-commit Self-verification):** AC #9's nx test web + Task 6's Playwright run satisfy. No backend changes → no Swagger regen.
- **Rule 16 (Test Assertion Quality):** AC #6 mandates render-count + testid stability assertions (specific matchers, no `toBeFalsy()`). AC #9 forbids new `as any` casts. Reuse `Partial<UseQueryResult<...>>` typed-mock pattern from bugfix-10-2 CR M3 (a recently established convention — verify in `apps/web/src/hooks/useDownloads.spec.ts:36-50`).
- **Rule 18 (API Boundary Case Transformation):** N/A — no new API boundaries.
- **Rule 20 (AC Contract Versioning):** AC #2 is `[@contract-v1]` because it introduces a NO-FLICKER invariant for the homepage. No upstream contract retroactively bound to "skeleton appears N times" — Story 10-5 set up the lazy-load infrastructure but did not contract the render-count. Future homepage changes that re-introduce flicker MUST bump this contract.

### Cross-Stack Split Check

- Backend tasks: 0
- Frontend tasks: 6 (Tasks 1-6 all FE; Task 7 is closeout / docs)
- Verdict: **Single story** — backend count is 0, well under the >3 threshold on either side.

### Implementation sketch — `useInViewport` lazy initializer (Task 2.1)

```typescript
export function useInViewport(
  ref: RefObject<Element | null>,
  { rootMargin = '0px', threshold = 0, once = true, disabled = false }: UseInViewportOptions = {}
): boolean {
  const [isInViewport, setIsInViewport] = useState<boolean>(() => {
    // Caller asserted visibility (eager block) — start visible, no observer.
    if (disabled) return true;
    // SSR / jsdom — start visible to avoid stranding content (matches existing
    // line 42-46 fallback semantics).
    if (typeof window === 'undefined' || typeof IntersectionObserver === 'undefined') {
      return true;
    }
    // Browser path — ref hasn't attached yet on first render, so we cannot
    // measure synchronously. Start `false` and let the effect's IO callback
    // promote to `true` on first intersection. NB: AC #3 acceptance is that
    // this still resolves to `true` SYNCHRONOUSLY for nodes already in
    // viewport — handled by the useLayoutEffect-based one-shot below.
    return false;
  });

  // One-shot synchronous measurement after first render but BEFORE paint.
  // Bypasses the IntersectionObserver round-trip for the in-viewport-at-mount
  // case (the most common case for above-the-fold lazy blocks). Falls through
  // to the long-lived observer for the not-yet-visible case.
  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    if (disabled || isInViewport) return;
    const node = ref.current;
    if (!node) return;
    const rect = node.getBoundingClientRect();
    const margin = parseInt(rootMargin, 10) || 0;
    const visible =
      rect.top < window.innerHeight + margin &&
      rect.bottom > -margin &&
      rect.left < window.innerWidth + margin &&
      rect.right > -margin;
    if (visible) setIsInViewport(true);
    // No cleanup needed — this is a one-shot synchronous read.
  }, [disabled, ref, rootMargin, isInViewport]);

  // Existing IntersectionObserver effect stays as the steady-state driver.
  useEffect(() => { /* ...unchanged from current implementation... */ }, [...]);

  return isInViewport;
}
```

### Test pattern — render-count assertion (Task 5.2)

```typescript
function renderCounter() {
  let count = 0;
  return {
    increment: () => (count += 1),
    get count() {
      return count;
    },
  };
}

// In test:
const counter = renderCounter();
function ExploreBlockSpy(props: ExploreBlockProps) {
  counter.increment();
  return <ExploreBlock {...props} />;
}

const { rerender } = render(<ExploreBlocksListInner blocks={mockBlocks} />);
await act(async () => {
  // simulate lazy-reveal IO callback by advancing time / firing observer
});
expect(counter.count).toBeLessThanOrEqual(3); // baseline; tighten per spike
```

### E2E pattern — no-flicker count snapshots (Task 6.2)

```typescript
test('[P1] should not re-mount any section skeleton after first paint', async ({ page }) => {
  // Mock all 6 endpoints with deterministic 100ms delays...
  await page.goto('/');

  const snapshots: number[] = [];
  for (const t of [50, 200, 500, 1500]) {
    await page.waitForTimeout(t);
    snapshots.push(await page.locator('[data-testid="hero-banner-skeleton"]').count());
  }
  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
  snapshots.push(await page.locator('[data-testid="hero-banner-skeleton"]').count());

  // Assert monotonically non-increasing (skeleton count never goes back up)
  for (let i = 1; i < snapshots.length; i++) {
    expect(snapshots[i]).toBeLessThanOrEqual(snapshots[i - 1]);
  }
  // And final state has the real content
  await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="hero-banner"]')).toBeVisible();
});
```

### Project Structure Notes

- Spike artifacts (`spike-bugfix-10-3-*.{md,json}`) live under `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/` alongside other story files. Profiler `.json` traces are large but worth keeping for a future regression compare.
- New E2E spec at `tests/e2e/homepage-no-flicker.spec.ts` mirrors the directory convention used by `tests/e2e/downloads.spec.ts`.
- New unit-test file location: prefer extending existing per-component `*.spec.tsx` over a new `__no-flicker.spec.tsx`. The new file is acceptable if cross-component setup is large (>50 lines of shared mocks).

### Previous-story intelligence

- **bugfix-10-1 (TMDb-ID 404)** — established the `Partial<ReturnType<...>>` typed-mock pattern (CR M3). Reuse, don't re-invent.
- **bugfix-10-2 (qBT HTTP status semantics)** — established the gate-on-`useQBittorrentConfig` pattern for `useDownloads`. Relevant here because `DownloadPanel`'s `isLoading = configLoading || (isConnected && downloadsLoading)` — the parent of our skeleton — depends on TWO sources, which adds an extra reactive cycle. AC #4.2 covers this.
- **Story 10-5 (homepage layout responsive)** — set up the four-section flex stack + `EAGER_BLOCK_COUNT = 2` lazy-load split. Read its Dev Notes ("Story 10-5 Task 2.3" comments throughout `ExploreBlock.tsx`, `useInViewport.ts`, `ExploreBlocksList.tsx`) before touching the eager/lazy logic.

### Git intelligence

- Recent commits: `7e7af95 chore(format)`, `5a46f93 test(downloads)`, `4f8f339 fix(downloads)`, `5dda924 fix(code-review): bugfix-10-1`, `2b1c7f5 fix(routes): bugfix-10-1`. Pattern: small focused commits, CR-fix commit always references prior `fix(...)` commit.
- Branching: this story's commits should chain off `main` (current HEAD `43cf108`). Use commit messages like:
  1. `spike(homepage): bugfix-10-3 React DevTools Profiler diagnosis — Bucket {A|B|C}` (Task 1.4)
  2. (if Bucket B/C) `fix(homepage): bugfix-10-3 useInViewport lazy initializer` (Task 2)
  3. (if Bucket B/C) `fix(homepage): bugfix-10-3 ExploreBlocksList stable container` (Task 3)
  4. (if Bucket B/C) `fix(homepage): bugfix-10-3 short-circuit empty before skeleton` (Task 4)
  5. (if Bucket B/C) `test(homepage): bugfix-10-3 render-count + testid no-flicker proof` (Tasks 5-6)
  6. (if Bucket A) `docs(project-context): bugfix-10-3 dev-mode StrictMode artifact` (Task 7.1)

### Change Log

| Date       | Change |
|------------|--------|
| 2026-04-20 | bugfix-10-3 filed in sprint-status.yaml as `backlog` with SPIKE-FIRST routing (≤15 min React DevTools profile before committing to a story). |
| 2026-05-07 | [@contract-v1] AC #2: NO-FLICKER invariant for homepage section skeletons — each section transitions skeleton → final-state AT MOST ONCE per cold load. Downstream consumers: future homepage changes (Story 10-X+) MUST not re-introduce flicker; the AC #6 render-count tests + AC #7 E2E count-snapshots are the regression guard. SM Bob `/create-story` (YOLO) generated comprehensive draft including spike-as-Task-1 + conditional fix tasks gated on AC #1 outcome (Bucket A: dev-mode-only → docs-only close; Bucket B/C: full fix). Story expanded vs. sprint-comment estimate (was "1-line QD or DEV story"); rationale: spike-first mandate + cross-component touch-points (4 components + 2 hooks + new E2E) warrant a structured story over an ad-hoc fix. |
